title: aphanes australis subsp. bonifaciensis
intro: Aphanes australis subsp. bonifaciensis, commonly known as the Australian aphanes or dwarf cinquefoil, is a plant species in the rose family (Rosaceae) that is native to the Australian region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Aphanes australis subsp. bonifaciensis include soothing skin irritations, reducing inflammation, and protecting against oxidative stress, making it a potential remedy for various health conditions. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
